docs.text: Linking soil fungi to bacterial community assembly in arid ecosystems ¶ Metadata ¶ Item_Type:: [[Article]] * Authors: [[Shuo Jiao]], [[Haiyan Chu]], [[Baogang Zhang]], [[Xiaorong Wei]], [[Weimin Chen]], [[Gehong Wei]] Year:: [[2022-02-24]] * Date Added: 2022-01-28 * URL: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/imt2.2 * DOI: 10.1002/imt2.2 * Cite key: jiao-LinkingSoilFungi-2022 , #zotero, #literature-notes, #reference, #bacteria, #16S * PDF Attachments - Jiao 等。 - 2022 - Linking soil fungi to bacterial community assembly.pdf * GitHub: https://github.com/shuojiao/Soil-fungi-drive-bacterial-community-assembly Abstract ¶ Revealing the roles of biotic factors in driving community assembly, which is crucial for the understanding of biodiversity and ecosystem functions, is a fundamental but infrequently investigated subject in microbial ecology. Here, combining a cross‐biome observational study with an experimental microcosm study, we provided evidence to reveal the major roles of biotic factors (i.e., soil fungi and cross‐kingdom species associations) in determining soil bacterial biogeography and community assembly in complex terrestrial ecosystems of the arid regions of northwest China. The results showed that the soil fungal richness mediates the balance of assembly processes of bacterial communities, and stochastic assembly processes decreased with increasing fungal richness. Our results further suggest that the predicted increase in aridity conditions due to climate change will reduce bacterial α‐diversity, particularly in desert soils and subsurface layer, and induce more negative species associations. Together, our study represents a significant advance in linking soil fungi to the mechanisms underlying bacterial biogeographic patterns and community assembly in arid ecosystems under climate aridity and land‐use change scenarios. 揭示驱动群落装配的生物因素对生物多样性和生态功能是至关重要的，在微生物生态领域是基础但很少研究的课题。这里结合交叉物种观测性研究，我们提供证据揭示了生物因子(土壤真菌和跨界物种联合)在确定土壤细菌生物地理和群落装配（位于中国西北干旱区的复杂大陆生态系统）的主要角色。 结果进一步说明由于气候变化导致的干旱条件的预测增加导致了细菌α多样性的减少，尤其是沙漠土和表面土，并且诱导了更多的负物种关联。总之，我们的研究表明在气候干旱和土地利用改变的情境下，将土壤真菌与干旱生态系统中的细菌生物地理模式和群落装配联系起来的方面取得了重大进展。 Design ¶ ![[../design/jiao-LinkingSoilFungi-2022]] Figs ¶ Content ¶ Zotero links ¶ Local library Cloud library
docs.title: Linking soil fungi to bacterial community assembly in arid ecosystems
Linking soil fungi to bacterial community assembly in arid ecosystems

docs.text: Item_Type:: [[Article]] * Authors: [[Shuo Jiao]], [[Haiyan Chu]], [[Baogang Zhang]], [[Xiaorong Wei]], [[Weimin Chen]], [[Gehong Wei]] Year:: [[2022-02-24]] * Date Added: 2022-01-28 * URL: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/imt2.2 * DOI: 10.1002/imt2.2 * Cite key: jiao-LinkingSoilFungi-2022 , #zotero, #literature-notes, #reference, #bacteria, #16S * PDF Attachments - Jiao 等。 - 2022 - Linking soil fungi to bacterial community assembly.pdf * GitHub: https://github.com/shuojiao/Soil-fungi-drive-bacterial-community-assembly
docs.title: Metadata

docs.text: Revealing the roles of biotic factors in driving community assembly, which is crucial for the understanding of biodiversity and ecosystem functions, is a fundamental but infrequently investigated subject in microbial ecology. Here, combining a cross‐biome observational study with an experimental microcosm study, we provided evidence to reveal the major roles of biotic factors (i.e., soil fungi and cross‐kingdom species associations) in determining soil bacterial biogeography and community assembly in complex terrestrial ecosystems of the arid regions of northwest China. The results showed that the soil fungal richness mediates the balance of assembly processes of bacterial communities, and stochastic assembly processes decreased with increasing fungal richness. Our results further suggest that the predicted increase in aridity conditions due to climate change will reduce bacterial α‐diversity, particularly in desert soils and subsurface layer, and induce more negative species associations. Together, our study represents a significant advance in linking soil fungi to the mechanisms underlying bacterial biogeographic patterns and community assembly in arid ecosystems under climate aridity and land‐use change scenarios. 揭示驱动群落装配的生物因素对生物多样性和生态功能是至关重要的，在微生物生态领域是基础但很少研究的课题。这里结合交叉物种观测性研究，我们提供证据揭示了生物因子(土壤真菌和跨界物种联合)在确定土壤细菌生物地理和群落装配（位于中国西北干旱区的复杂大陆生态系统）的主要角色。 结果进一步说明由于气候变化导致的干旱条件的预测增加导致了细菌α多样性的减少，尤其是沙漠土和表面土，并且诱导了更多的负物种关联。总之，我们的研究表明在气候干旱和土地利用改变的情境下，将土壤真菌与干旱生态系统中的细菌生物地理模式和群落装配联系起来的方面取得了重大进展。

docs.text: Coupling Bacterial Community Assembly to Microbial Metabolism across Soil Profiles ¶ ^81f04c Metadata ¶ Item_Type:: [[Article]] * Authors: [[Lu Luan]], [[Chao Liang]], [[Lijun Chen]], [[Haotian Wang]], [[Qinsong Xu]], [[Yuji Jiang]], [[Bo Sun]] Year:: [[2020-06-30]] * Date Added: 2022-01-27 * URL: https://journals.asm.org/doi/10.1128/mSystems.00298-20 * DOI: 10.1128/mSystems.00298-20 * Cite key: luan-CouplingBacterialCommunity-2020 , #zotero, #literature-notes, #reference, #bacteria, #16S * PDF Attachments - Luan 等。 - 2020 - Coupling Bacterial Community Assembly to Microbial.pdf Abstract ¶ Soil microbial community assembly is crucial for understanding the mechanisms of microbial communities that regulate ecosystem-level functioning. The relative contributions of stochastic and deterministic processes to microbial community assembly remain poorly deﬁned, and major questions exist concerning the soil organic carbon (SOC) dynamics of microbial community assembly in deep soil. Here, the bacterial community assembly processes were explored across ﬁve soil proﬁle depths (up to 80 cm) during a 15-year ﬁeld experiment involving four fertilization regimes. We found that the bacterial community assembly was initially governed by deterministic selection in topsoil but was progressively structured by increasing stochastic dispersal with depth. The migration rate (m) and ␤-null deviation pattern supported the hypothesis of a relatively greater inﬂuence of dispersal in deep soil, which was correlated with bacterial community assembly by stochastic processes. These changes in the entire community assembly reﬂected consistent assembly processes of the two most dominant phyla, Acidobacteria and Chloroﬂexi. Structural equation modeling showed that soil features (pH and total phosphorus) and bacterial interactions (competition and network complexity) were signiﬁcantly related to bacterial community assembly in the 0-to-10-cm and 10-to-20-cm layers. Partial Mantel tests, structural equation modeling, and random forest modeling consistently indicated a strong and signiﬁcant correlation between bacterial community assemblages and SOC dynamics, implying that bacterial assembly processes would potentially suppress SOC metabolism and mineralization when the contributions of stochastic dispersal to communities increased in deeper layers. Our results have important implications for integrating bacterial community assembly processes into the predictions of SOC dynamics. ^e8957e Tips ¶ dff * Design ¶ ![[design/luan-CouplingBacterialCommunity-2020]] Figs ¶ Content ¶ ![[content/luan-CouplingBacterialCommunity-2020]] Zotero links ¶ Local library Cloud library
docs.title: Coupling Bacterial Community Assembly to Microbial Metabolism across Soil Profiles
Coupling Bacterial Community Assembly to Microbial Metabolism across Soil Profiles

docs.title: Metadata

docs.text: Soil microbial community assembly is crucial for understanding the mechanisms of microbial communities that regulate ecosystem-level functioning. The relative contributions of stochastic and deterministic processes to microbial community assembly remain poorly deﬁned, and major questions exist concerning the soil organic carbon (SOC) dynamics of microbial community assembly in deep soil. Here, the bacterial community assembly processes were explored across ﬁve soil proﬁle depths (up to 80 cm) during a 15-year ﬁeld experiment involving four fertilization regimes. We found that the bacterial community assembly was initially governed by deterministic selection in topsoil but was progressively structured by increasing stochastic dispersal with depth. The migration rate (m) and ␤-null deviation pattern supported the hypothesis of a relatively greater inﬂuence of dispersal in deep soil, which was correlated with bacterial community assembly by stochastic processes. These changes in the entire community assembly reﬂected consistent assembly processes of the two most dominant phyla, Acidobacteria and Chloroﬂexi. Structural equation modeling showed that soil features (pH and total phosphorus) and bacterial interactions (competition and network complexity) were signiﬁcantly related to bacterial community assembly in the 0-to-10-cm and 10-to-20-cm layers. Partial Mantel tests, structural equation modeling, and random forest modeling consistently indicated a strong and signiﬁcant correlation between bacterial community assemblages and SOC dynamics, implying that bacterial assembly processes would potentially suppress SOC metabolism and mineralization when the contributions of stochastic dispersal to communities increased in deeper layers. Our results have important implications for integrating bacterial community assembly processes into the predictions of SOC dynamics. ^e8957e

docs.text: 实地调查 ¶ 取样 ¶ 2017年7-8月取样，126个点，每个点3个plots，分为深层(15-30cm)和浅层(0-15cm)。包含37个农业，28个森林，15个湿地，26个草地，20个沙漠。沿河西走廊。一个沙漠表层样品DNA提取失败，共251个样品。 土壤性质 ¶ 常规指标：pH, moisture, cation exchange capacity(CEC)阳离子交换量, SOC, DOC, TN, NO 3 , NH 4 , TP, AP, TK, AK. 气候数据：来自 Worldclim database . 干旱指数(AI): 1-降水/蒸发量，来自 Global Potential Evapotranspiration database . 测序 ¶ 细菌16S v4-v5区 真菌18S ITS1 Microcosm study ¶ 土壤来自于中国西北的干旱区，2019年8月取aridity=0.663草地，0-15cm表层土。土样过2mm筛，气候室25℃平衡数周。然后移至塑料盆，用杀菌剂(环乙酰亚胺)修正， 三个梯度0(D0)，6ppm(D1)，14ppm(D2)，四个重复 ，共12个样品。20℃培养60天。 分析 ¶ Spatial mapping of bacterial diversity ¶ 预测细菌Shannon多样性，co-kriging interpolation(automap), 5个环境指标：soil properties(soil C and pH), climate(AI and MAT). Cross-validation 基于pearson相关系数（automap::autoKrige.cv）。 土壤属性来自 ISRIC (global gridded soil information) Soil Grids. Correlation Network ¶ 超过10%样品中出现的ASV，|Spearman's p | > 0.6 & p < 0.001 DDRs ¶ 1-bray_curtis Null model ¶ βNTI，Stegen的过程。 Statistical ¶ 随机森林(rfPermute)，多元线性回归(stats::lm)，方差分解(relaimpo::calc.relimp)，基于R 2 的前向选择(vegan::ordiR2step)，CAP(vegan::capscale), standard/partial Mantel test(ecodist::mantel)，NMDS(vegan::metaMDS)。
docs.title: jiao LinkingSoilFungi 2022

docs.text: Fig1 ¶ ^c0ed02 所有刨面的环境变异和SOC动态( Turkey's post hoc test) A. 所有土壤性质的整体变异(方差-协方差矩阵) B. M0: 不施肥，M1：少施，M2：多施，M3：多施+石灰 C. average well color development(AWCD)不知道什么东西，分为六大类：碳水化合物、羧酸、氨基酸、聚合物、酚酸和胺 Fig2 ¶ 细菌群落装配(phyla水平，Turkey's post hoc test) A. weighted beta nearest taxon index(βNTI) B. 周转率，分为五种：确定过程（同质选择、变异选择），随机过程（扩散限制、随机扩散）和未确定 C. 细菌群落的生态位宽度 D. β多样性的0模型偏差将群落装配分为确定性和随机性过程。偏差接近0表示越高的随机性 Fig3 ¶ 细菌共线性网络。 边的|相关性|>0.6且p值<0.01，蓝色是正相关，红色是负相关。颜色代表phylum/class。点大小代表degree。 Fig4 ¶ Fig5 ¶
docs.title: Fig1

docs.text: Fig1 ¶ 土壤细菌多样性和和共线网络的整体格局 A. 香农指数预测分布 B. 细菌门水平不同生物群落的分布，ribbon厚度表示相对丰度，spearman相关性 C. 基于干旱偏好的跨界网络，大小表示degree D. Degree与环境因子的相关性(pearson?)。AI与degree的线性关系(非线性关系？) Fig2 ¶ 不同生物群落中细菌α多样性的驱动因子 A. 基于相关性(spearman，颜色表示)和随机森林(大小表示MSE)的生物/非生物因素对α多样性的关系。AD：平均degree，Neg：负相关百分比，SR：土壤真菌richness B. 细菌与主要驱动因子的最小二乘法拟合，subfig是标准化斜率(mean±SEM) C. 在表层和亚表层Shannon与AI的关系 Fig3 ¶ 细菌β多样性的生物地理模式 A. CAP，颜色代表AD B. DDR C. 细菌周转率，同质选择HS，异质选择VS，随机过程Sto Fig4 ¶ 优势细菌的主要分布及主要驱动因素(相对丰度前10%，且存在于所有样品)。 Heatmap图基于多元线性回归指定ASV的方差分解 Bar图，解释变异的最重要变量的百分比 Box图，生物/非生物因素解释的比例 Fig5 ¶ 真菌richness对形成土壤细菌群落的装配过程的影响 A. βNTI，将真菌richness分成14组 B. 将真菌richness分成21组 C. 随机性过程和确定性过程, 分成14组 D. 随机性和确定性过程，分成21组 Fig6 ¶ 微宇宙中实验中真菌richness与细菌群落装配的关系 A. richness B. 装配过程的变化 C. NMDS D. 标准化(z-score)后的丰度图，Prop 差异物种的百分比，RA 差异物种的总丰度 Fig S1 ¶ 优势细菌的差异比较 Fig S2 ¶ 每个土壤样本子网的拓扑属性驱动因子 A. 随机森林预测，5000树，MSE B. 通过多元线性回归和变差分解 C. 干旱与网络属性的二阶最小二乘拟合 Fig S3 ¶ 不同杀菌剂浓度的细菌α多样性
